Fake display functions for initialization, clearing, updating, entering static mode, and pulsing VCOM.

# Purpose
This code is a C source file that provides a set of function stubs for managing a display interface. It includes the header file `fake_display.h` and defines five functions: [`display_init`](<#display_init>), [`display_clear`](<#display_clear>), [`display_update`](<#display_update>), [`display_enter_static`](<#display_enter_static>), and [`display_pulse_vcom`](<#display_pulse_vcom>). Each function is currently implemented as an empty function, indicating that they are placeholders for future development. The functions are intended to initialize the display, clear the display, update the display with callbacks, enter a static display mode, and pulse the VCOM (Voltage Common) signal, respectively.

- `fake_display.h`
